Group tool chunks by category to reduce network requests

Add manualChunks to Vite config to bundle tool components by category
(generate, encode, convert, hash, text) instead of one chunk per
component. Reduces JS chunks from 74 to 26, so category pages load
one file instead of 10+ separate requests on first visit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: scheduled custom actions create tasks directly instead of LLM pipeline

The LLM pipeline was unreliable for scheduled commands — it picked wrong
machines and wrong shell commands. Now natural language scheduled actions
create tasks directly via TaskService, which the dispatcher routes to the
correct machine with Claude Code handling the actual execution.

Slash commands (e.g. /repos) still use direct action dispatch.

Also:
- heartbeat() falls back to upsert when machine missing
- API routes resolve alias machine_ids to canonical SSH targets

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(agentic): add SSE streaming support to agentic-model client

Enable Server-Sent Events streaming for OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoints,
reducing time-to-first-token while keeping the inter-component protocol
unchanged (complete AgentResponse messages over JetStream).

- Add Stream field to model.EndpointConfig for per-endpoint opt-in
- Implement streamChatCompletion with tool call delta aggregation
- Add StreamChunk type and ChunkHandler callback for real-time chunks
- Add Prometheus metrics: stream_chunks_total, stream_ttft_seconds
- Wire chunk handler in component for fire-and-forget NATS publishing
- Add SSE streaming to mock OpenAI server for e2e testing
- Add verify-streaming-metrics stage to e2e agentic scenario
- Document Ollama chat_template_kwargs limitation on Options field

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: use curl_cffi for Substack Notes POST to bypass Cloudflare WAF

Cloudflare Bot Management blocks POST requests from Python's requests
library due to TLS fingerprint mismatch. Switch to curl_cffi which
impersonates Chrome's TLS handshake. Also send bodyJson as dict (not
json.dumps string) per Substack's expected format, with string fallback
on 422. Session warm-up via GET /notes collects required CF cookies.

Tested live: note ID 222976874 posted successfully.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

add unimplemented-specification feedback loop to kilroy:land and implement node

When holdout verification finds failures, kilroy:land now triages each into
spec-gap (needs critique/revision) vs unimplemented-spec (spec is correct but
implementation doesn't follow it). Unimplemented specs are written to
.ai/unimplemented_specifications.md with direct spec references. The implement
prompt treats this as a repair artifact alongside postmortem_latest.md.

Includes v62 holdout failure analysis: 4 unimplemented spec entries covering
lifecycle turn processing, declared_type field access, error loop heuristic,
and pipeline state reset.

commit.message:
Add atomic writes and file locking for secrets file (#3998)

The secrets file used os.WriteFile directly, which is non-atomic — a
crash mid-write could leave a corrupted file. It also had no file
locking, so concurrent processes could race on writes.

- Replace os.WriteFile with fileutils.AtomicWriteFile (temp file +
  fsync + rename) in the encrypted secrets manager
- Wrap SetSecret, DeleteSecret, and Cleanup with file locking using
  lockfile.NewTrackedLock to prevent cross-process races
- Extract shared WithFileLock helper into pkg/fileutils/lock.go and
  reuse it from both pkg/secrets and pkg/client
- Update concurrency test to verify file-level consistency by reloading
  from disk after concurrent writes

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: change avg replies per merchant to merchant response frequency

Update "Average Replies Per Merchant" to show how many times each
responding merchant replied, rather than AI replies sent per merchant.

Before: Total AI replies sent / Total merchants (all)
After: Total inbound emails / Unique merchants who responded

Example:
- Merchant A: sent 2 inbound emails
- Merchant B: sent 1 inbound email
- Avg replies per merchant = 3 / 2 = 1.5

This better reflects merchant engagement and response patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix outbound caller ID: set CID before bridging to gateway

Three issues caused outbound calls to show as anonymous:

1. Outbound route dialplan never set caller ID before bridge action.
   Now sets effective_caller_id based on route's cid_mode:
   - "extension" (default): uses extension's outbound CID variables
   - "trunk": uses trunk's username as CID
   - "custom": uses route's custom_cid field

2. Directory user only set outbound_caller_id variables when
   external_cid_name/number were explicitly configured. Now always
   sets them with fallback chain: external → internal → extension number.

3. Added _get_trunk_cid helper for trunk CID mode lookups.

Also fixes pre-existing test assertion (sip_username mismatch).

commit.message:
Add tools section, mermaid diagrams, fix numeric errors from full audit

New content:
- Tools section (6 pages): overview, mempool, tx-decoder, block-analyzer,
  fee-tracker, next-block — each documents a Python tool with protocol context
- UTXO lifecycle Mermaid diagram in Ch01
- mdbook-mermaid preprocessor support in book.toml and CI workflow

Numeric fixes from full 12-chapter audit:
- Ch05: "50 billionth" → "5 billionth" (50 BTC = 5B sats, not 50B)
- Ch10: Solo mining times all wrong by ~2.5x (1 S21 Pro: 26yr → 65yr)
- Ch11: SPV header chain size ~60 MB → ~75 MB (for 2026 block heights)
- Ch12: 42 blocks = 7 hours, not 7.5 hours
- examples/pizza-tx: Fee was ~0.01% of value, not "nearly 10%"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: simplify search_session_history — drop query, add per-run limits (#6858)

## Summary

Simplifies the `search_session_history` feature by removing fragile
substring query matching and letting the LLM decide relevance from
session previews.

- **Rename** `search_past_sessions_limit` → `num_past_sessions` for
clarity
- **Add** `num_past_session_runs` config (default 3) to control how many
runs per session appear in previews
- **Remove** `query` param from `search_past_sessions` — the tool now
lists recent sessions with per-run user/assistant previews, and the LLM
picks which to read
- **Delete** `_search_session_messages` helper (no longer needed)
- **Update** `_extract_session_preview` to return `{session_id,
created_at, runs: [{user, assistant}]}` format

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] Model update
- [ ] Other:

---

## Checklist

- [x] Code complies with style guidelines
- [x] Ran format/validation scripts (`./scripts/format.sh` and
`./scripts/validate.sh`)
- [x] Self-review completed
- [x] Documentation updated (comments, docstrings)
- [x] Examples and guides: Relevant cookbook examples have been included
or updated (if applicable)
- [x] Tested in clean environment
- [x] Tests added/updated (if applicable)

---

## Additional Notes

- All 19 unit tests pass. Format and validate scripts pass cleanly.
- Both agent and team cookbooks updated.

commit.message:
fix: replace DraggableScrollableSheet with simple SizedBox layout

DraggableScrollableSheet was causing persistent drag-to-dismiss
issues on iOS — the sheet would freeze mid-drag and become
permanently stuck. Replaced with a simple SizedBox(height: 92%)
+ Column layout. The showModalBottomSheet built-in enableDrag
now handles dismiss natively via the drag handle area, which
is reliably outside the scrollable content.

fix: await Supabase saves before navigating to prevent data loss

All save functions (saveProfile, saveCheckIn, saveEpisode, updateEpisode)

surface errors to the user rather than silently swallowing them.

commit.message:
feat: add dependency-aware backlog skill — deps graph, blocked/unblocked scoring (#103)

The /backlog skill now tracks dependencies between items using a canonical
## Dependencies section in issue bodies (- Blocks: / - Blocked by:).

Changes to SKILL.md:
- New "Dependency tracking" section with canonical format and scanning procedure
- New /backlog deps subcommand for viewing the dependency graph
- Dashboard splits items into Top Ready / Blocked sections
- /backlog next excludes blocked items, adds +15 unblock bonus per downstream item
- /backlog add writes deps bidirectionally to both issues
- /backlog done cleans up blocker refs on downstream issues
- /backlog drop warns before dropping items that block others
- /backlog update supports blocks= and blocked-by= field syntax

Also migrated 5 existing issues (#95, #93, #98, #101, #64) to canonical format.

commit.message:
Use auto-discovery only — remove explicit pipeline file collection

Pipeline files (databricks.yml, spark-pipeline.yml) are now only collected
through auto-discovery, not by passing them as CLI arguments. This avoids
collisions with other pytest plugins and settings.

- pytest_configure discovers files and flags them in _sdp_discovered_files
- pytest_collect_file only collects pipeline files present in that set
- Remove duplicate string-library handler in _discover_unit_spec_files
- Update README to remove explicit file passing examples
